pi_items.source: Task setup script and file system verification at /home/ga/Documents/captures/smtp.pcap
tshark protocol analysis: 32 SMTP frames confirmed in TCP stream with EHLO greeting, AUTH LOGIN, MAIL FROM, RCPT TO, and SMTP response codes (220, 250, 334, 235)
Task description and export_result.sh script which explicitly references this filename
install_wireshark.sh downloads from official URLs with fallbacks to GitLab mirror
File system verification and tshark inspection of downloaded smtp.pcap
tshark -r smtp.pcap -Y 'smtp' | wc -l analysis; 28 frames in io,phs hierarchy
TCP stream contains '220-xc90.websitewelcome.com ESMTP' greeting and EHLO command
TCP stream contains 10+ SMTP response codes (220, 250, 334, 235, 221) confirming server responses
file command output: 'pcap capture file, microsecond ts (little-endian) - version 2.4 (Ethernet, capture length 65535)'

privileged_info_summary: The smtp.pcap file is a legitimate, publicly available sample capture from Wireshark's official Sample Captures repository (https://wiki.wireshark.org/SampleCaptures). The 27,850-byte file contains 60 total packets with 32 SMTP protocol frames demonstrating a complete SMTP email transaction. The TCP stream (15,614 characters) contains all expected SMTP elements: ESMTP greeting (220 response), EHLO command, AUTH LOGIN authentication, MAIL FROM command, RCPT TO command, and SMTP response codes (220, 250, 334, 235). The conversation reconstructs a full email exchange from client (10.10.1.4:1470) to server (74.53.140.153:25) using Exim SMTP server. All metadata claims (pcap_file: 'smtp.pcap', expected_protocol: 'SMTP', output_file: 'smtp_stream.txt') are accurate.
